joke: I told my computer I needed a break, and it said: “No problem, I’ll go to sleep.”
Why did the bicycle fall over? It was two-tired.
Why did the scarecrow win an award? Because he was outstanding in his field.
Why did the mushroom get invited to the party? Because he was a fungi.
Why don’t skeletons fight each other? They don’t have the guts.
Why did the coffee file a police report? It got mugged.
Why did the calendar get promoted? It had all the right dates.
Why did the tomato blush? Because it saw the salad dressing.
Why did the moon go to school? To get a little brighter.
Why did the pencil break up with the eraser? It felt wiped out.
Why did the umbrella get a raise? It really knew how to handle pressure.
Why did the duck bring a suitcase? It was going on a quack-cation.
Why did the lamp get invited to the party? It really knew how to light up a room.
Why did the sock get promoted? It really had the right sole.
Why did the math book look sad? Because it had too many problems.
What do you call a fish with no eyes? Fsh.
I bought a chair that doubles as a ladder. It really has its ups and downs.
Why did the piano get locked out? It lost its keys.
Why did the detective bring a ladder? To get to the bottom of the case.
Why did the volcano get invited to every party? Because it really knew how to bring the heat.
Why did the barber win the race? He knew all the short cuts.
I used to be indecisive. Now I’m not so sure.
Why did the faucet blush? Because it saw the plumber.
Why don’t atoms ever tell secrets? Because they make up everything.
Why did the stapler get the raise? It really kept things together.
Why did the painter bring a ladder? He wanted to reach new heights.
I asked my cat for a loan. She said, “You’ve got to be kitten me.”
Why did the elevator break up with the stairs? It needed space.
Why did the mirror get promoted? It had great reflection.
Why did the envelope blush? It saw the stamp.
